description: Found this a.m. along with Bill Maynard. Previously reported birds, there were at least four Sprague's Pipits in the short grass prairie. The only other species at this location that morning were Horned Larks and Chestnut-collared Longspurs. The pipits showed in an undulating climb that went much higher than the Horned Larks, and then they would dive down to the grass. There was an accompanying flight call. The birds had all the markings shown in the Nat Geo 6th edition including the smallish head and corresponding large eye. One photo (courtesy of Bill Maynard) is available on request.
